name: Stripe Invoicing
description:
What Stripe Does

Sometimes you don't need a "platform"; you just need a "pipe" to get money from the client's bank to yours. That’s Stripe Invoicing. ​It provides a reliable, no-frills way to send invoices and collect recurring payments. If you already use Stripe to process payments, this is the native way to handle the paperwork.

Source: Stripe

Key Features

​🏆 Hosted Invoice Pages: Stripe hosts the payment page, so you don't have to worry about security.

🏆 Smart Retries: Uses data from millions of businesses to retry cards when they are most likely to succeed.

​🏆 Automatic Reconciliation: Matches incoming wire transfers to open invoices.

​Benefits of Stripe

​It is one of the most "stable" systems on the planet. You never have to worry about it being down, and the integration with other tools (via Zapier) is endless.

​Pricing

  • ​Starter: 0.4% per paid invoice.
  • ​Plus: 0.5% per paid invoice (includes Smart Retries and Quotes).

Note: This is in addition to the standard ~2.9% credit card processing fee.

​Pros

​✅ Flawless mechanics; payments just work.

✅ No monthly subscription fee (pay-as-you-go).

​Cons

​🚫 No "client portal" for them to see their history or results.

🚫 The backend is built for developers, not account managers.

‍
what_people_say:
​What Customers Say

​"Stripe Invoicing is a terrific component of a confusing system... the backend support is awful when it can be found."

– Review collected by and hosted on G2.com.
